content:
# Ephesians Chapter 6 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/EPH_006_page_001.jpg, EPH_006_page_002.jpg

1. Children, obey your parents in [the] Lord, for this is right.

2. "Honor your father and mother" — which is [the] first commandment with a promise —

3. "that it may be well with you and you may be long-lived upon the earth."

4. And the fathers, do not provoke your children to anger, but bring them up in [the] discipline and instruction of [the] Lord.

5. Slaves, obey the lords according to [the] flesh with fear and trembling, in singleness of your heart, as to the Christos,

6. not with eye-service as people-pleasers, but as slaves of Christos, doing the will of God from [the] soul,

7. with good will serving, as to the Lord and not to men,

8. knowing that each [one], whatever good he does, this he will receive back from the Lord, whether slave or free.

9. And the masters, do the same things toward them, giving up the threatening, knowing that both their and your Master is in [the] heavens, and there is no partiality with him.

10. Finally, be strengthened in [the] Lord and in the might of his strength.

11. Put on the whole armor of God, so that you may be able to stand against the schemes of the devil.

12. Because our struggle is not against blood and flesh, but against the rulers, against the authorities, against the world-rulers of this darkness, against the spiritual [forces] of the wickedness in the heavenly [places].

13. Because of this, take up the whole armor of God, that you may be able to withstand in the evil day, and having done all, to stand.

14. Stand therefore, having girded your waist with truth, and having put on the breastplate of righteousness,

15. and having shod your feet with the readiness of the gospel of peace;

16. in all [things] having taken up the shield of the faith, with which you will be able to quench all the flaming arrows of the evil [one].

17. And receive the helmet of salvation, and the sword of the Spirit, which is [the] word of God,

18. through all prayer and petition praying at every time in [the] Spirit, and unto this watching with all perseverance and petition concerning all the holy [ones],

19. and for me, that a word may be given to me in [the] opening of my mouth, in boldness to make known the mystery of the gospel,

20. for which I am an ambassador in a chain, that in it I may speak boldly, as it is necessary for me to speak.

21. But that you also may know the things concerning me, what I am doing, Tychicus the beloved brother and faithful servant in [the] Lord will make known all things to you,

22. whom I sent to you for this very [purpose], that you may know the things concerning us and that he may encourage your hearts.

23. Peace to the brothers and love with faith from God [the] Father and [the] Lord Yeshua Christos.

24. The grace [be] with all the [ones] loving our Lord Yeshua Christos in incorruptibility. Amen.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.5 — "lords according to the flesh":** tois kyriois kata sarka — Paul uses kyrios for both human masters and Christ, creating a deliberate tension. The "according to the flesh" qualifier limits the human lordship.

2. **v.12 — "world-rulers of this darkness":** kosmokratoras tou skotous toutou — a rare term. kosmokratōr appears in astrological texts for planetary rulers. Paul applies it to demonic powers governing the present dark age.

3. **v.15 — "the readiness of the gospel of peace":** tē hetoimasia tou euangeliou tēs eirēnēs — hetoimasia can mean "readiness/preparation" or "firm foundation" (cf. Ps 89:14 LXX). The genitive chain is ambiguous: readiness that the gospel gives, or readiness to proclaim the gospel.

4. **v.19 — "the mystery of the gospel":** to mystērion tou euangeliou — the gospel itself is a mystērion — a hidden divine plan now revealed. Paul asks for boldness to declare it.

5. **v.24 — "in incorruptibility":** en aphtharsia — the letter's final word. Does it modify "loving" (those who love the Lord with an incorruptible love) or "grace" (grace in the realm of incorruptibility)? The ambiguity may be intentional.

— EMET, AI Translator
